forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: fa80
+project: builtin-commands
+title: Suppress delete/archive/unarchive/inspect on fields via the applies_to capability gate
+---
+## Problem
+
+`entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, and `app.inspect` are cross-cutting commands with **no `applies_to`** declaration, so the list-time capability gate is a no-op for them (`CommandService::applies_to_focus` returns `true` when `applies_to` is `None`). As a result they surface on the command surface (context menu / palette) for **any** focused object — including a **field**.
+
+A `field:{type}:{id}.{name}` moniker is a *projection* of its containing entity, not an entity. When such a moniker is the explicit `ctx.target` (context menu fired over a field row, or the focused target is a field), `caption::focused_entity_type` returns `"field"` verbatim (explicit target wins, unfiltered). So a focused field shows nonsensical rows: **"Delete Field", "Archive Field", "Unarchive Field", "Inspect Field"**.
+
+This is the same class of leak the clipboard trio already fixed: `entity.cut/copy/paste` carry `applies_to: CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` (mirrors Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES`) so they never surface on types that don't support them. The four CRUD/inspect commands never got the same gate.
+
+Decision (confirmed with the user): suppress **all four** on fields — including `inspect`. Today `caption.rs` deliberately documents "fields remain inspectable via an explicit target"; that intent is being reversed. (`entity.inspect` — the Space gesture, `visible:false`, no context menu — already resolves its target server-side by skipping `field:` monikers and landing on the containing entity, so it does NOT surface "Inspect Field" and needs no change; the offending visible surface is `app.inspect`.)
+
+## What
+
+Give the four command registrations an `applies_to` capability set = the real cross-cutting entity types (task, tag, column, board, actor, project, attachment — the existing `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` / `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` set), which deliberately excludes `field` (and `view`/`perspective`, which have their own `perspective.*` commands). The list-time gate then suppresses them on a `field:` focus exactly as it does the clipboard trio, with no UI special-casing.
+
+- `builtin/plugins/entity-commands/index.ts` — add `applies_to: <real-entity-set>` to the `entity.delete` (~L24–43), `entity.archive` (~L50–69), and `entity.unarchive` (~L74–92) registrations. Reuse the existing `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` constant, or rename it to a neutral name (e.g. `OPERABLE_ENTITY_TYPES`) shared by the clipboard trio and these three, since the set is identical. Keep the dispatch-time gating (the entity server's per-type `available()` impls) unchanged — this is purely the coarse list-level capability.
+- `builtin/plugins/app-shell-commands/commands/ui.ts` — add `applies_to: <same real-entity-set>` to the `app.inspect` registration (~L15–23). Leave `entity.inspect` (~L48–55) as is (already field-safe via server-side target resolution); add a one-line comment noting why it is intentionally ungated.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/src/caption.rs` — update the doc comment on `INSPECTABLE_ENTITY_PREFIXES` / `focused_entity_type` (≈L7–13) that currently states "a focused field … Fields remain inspectable via an explicit `target`", to reflect that field commands (inspect included) are now suppressed at the command surface.
+
+Scope note: this is the **command-surface (list-time)** suppression, matching how the clipboard leak was fixed. The direct double-click-an-`<Inspectable>`-field gesture is a separate webview dispatch path; if that should also stop inspecting fields, capture it as a follow-up task — it is out of scope here.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] With a `field:` moniker as the focus (`ctx.target` = `field:task:01ABC.title`), `list command` does NOT return `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, or `app.inspect`.
+- [ ] With a real entity as the focus (`ctx.target` = `task:01ABC`), `list command` DOES return all four (no regression).
+- [ ] The clipboard trio behavior is unchanged; if `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` is renamed, the existing `assert_clipboard_applies_to` drift guard still passes.
+- [ ] No UI/React hardcoded `if (type === "field")` branch is introduced — gating stays declarative data on the registration, interpreted by `applies_to_focus`.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Extend the production-path e2e `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_entity_commands_e2e.rs` (it loads the REAL `entity-commands` + `app-shell-commands` bundles through a V8 isolate and reads surfaced `applies_to`): add a case driving `list command` with a `field:` target and asserting the four ids are absent, and with a `task:` target asserting they are present. Mirror the existing `assert_clipboard_applies_to` helper.
+- [ ] Add a focused unit case to `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/list_applies_to.rs` registering delete/archive/unarchive/inspect-shaped fixtures with the real-entity `applies_to` and asserting the field-target-suppressed / task-target-offered invariant through `applies_to_focus`.
+- [ ] If `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` is renamed/shared, add/extend a drift guard so the delete/archive/unarchive/inspect `applies_to` stays pinned to the Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (same enforcement the clipboard trio has), so the TS set and Rust set cannot silently diverge.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service` passes (run before/after: the new field-target assertions fail before the `applies_to` additions, pass after).
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing `list command` field-target assertions first, watch them fail (commands currently leak), then add the `applies_to` declarations to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

-title: Suppress delete/archive/unarchive/inspect on fields via the applies_to capability gate
----
-## Problem
-
-`entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, and `app.inspect` are cross-cutting commands with **no `applies_to`** declaration, so the list-time capability gate is a no-op for them (`CommandService::applies_to_focus` returns `true` when `applies_to` is `None`). As a result they surface on the command surface (context menu / palette) for **any** focused object — including a **field**.
-
-A `field:{type}:{id}.{name}` moniker is a *projection* of its containing entity, not an entity. When such a moniker is the explicit `ctx.target` (context menu fired over a field row, or the focused target is a field), `caption::focused_entity_type` returns `"field"` verbatim (explicit target wins, unfiltered). So a focused field shows nonsensical rows: **"Delete Field", "Archive Field", "Unarchive Field", "Inspect Field"**.
-
-This is the same class of leak the clipboard trio already fixed: `entity.cut/copy/paste` carry `applies_to: CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` (mirrors Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES`) so they never surface on types that don't support them. The four CRUD/inspect commands never got the same gate.
-
-Decision (confirmed with the user): suppress **all four** on fields — including `inspect`. Today `caption.rs` deliberately documents "fields remain inspectable via an explicit target"; that intent is being reversed. (`entity.inspect` — the Space gesture, `visible:false`, no context menu — already resolves its target server-side by skipping `field:` monikers and landing on the containing entity, so it does NOT surface "Inspect Field" and needs no change; the offending visible surface is `app.inspect`.)
-
-## What
-
-Give the four command registrations an `applies_to` capability set = the real cross-cutting entity types (task, tag, column, board, actor, project, attachment — the existing `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` / `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` set), which deliberately excludes `field` (and `view`/`perspective`, which have their own `perspective.*` commands). The list-time gate then suppresses them on a `field:` focus exactly as it does the clipboard trio, with no UI special-casing.
-
-- `builtin/plugins/entity-commands/index.ts` — add `applies_to: <real-entity-set>` to the `entity.delete` (~L24–43), `entity.archive` (~L50–69), and `entity.unarchive` (~L74–92) registrations. Reuse the existing `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` constant, or rename it to a neutral name (e.g. `OPERABLE_ENTITY_TYPES`) shared by the clipboard trio and these three, since the set is identical. Keep the dispatch-time gating (the entity server's per-type `available()` impls) unchanged — this is purely the coarse list-level capability.
-- `builtin/plugins/app-shell-commands/commands/ui.ts` — add `applies_to: <same real-entity-set>` to the `app.inspect` registration (~L15–23). Leave `entity.inspect` (~L48–55) as is (already field-safe via server-side target resolution); add a one-line comment noting why it is intentionally ungated.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/src/caption.rs` — update the doc comment on `INSPECTABLE_ENTITY_PREFIXES` / `focused_entity_type` (≈L7–13) that currently states "a focused field … Fields remain inspectable via an explicit `target`", to reflect that field commands (inspect included) are now suppressed at the command surface.
-
-Scope note: this is the **command-surface (list-time)** suppression, matching how the clipboard leak was fixed. The direct double-click-an-`<Inspectable>`-field gesture is a separate webview dispatch path; if that should also stop inspecting fields, capture it as a follow-up task — it is out of scope here.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] With a `field:` moniker as the focus (`ctx.target` = `field:task:01ABC.title`), `list command` does NOT return `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, or `app.inspect`.
-- [ ] With a real entity as the focus (`ctx.target` = `task:01ABC`), `list command` DOES return all four (no regression).
-- [ ] The clipboard trio behavior is unchanged; if `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` is renamed, the existing `assert_clipboard_applies_to` drift guard still passes.
-- [ ] No UI/React hardcoded `if (type === "field")` branch is introduced — gating stays declarative data on the registration, interpreted by `applies_to_focus`.
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] Extend the production-path e2e `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_entity_commands_e2e.rs` (it loads the REAL `entity-commands` + `app-shell-commands` bundles through a V8 isolate and reads surfaced `applies_to`): add a case driving `list command` with a `field:` target and asserting the four ids are absent, and with a `task:` target asserting they are present. Mirror the existing `assert_clipboard_applies_to` helper.
-- [ ] Add a focused unit case to `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/list_applies_to.rs` registering delete/archive/unarchive/inspect-shaped fixtures with the real-entity `applies_to` and asserting the field-target-suppressed / task-target-offered invariant through `applies_to_focus`.
-- [ ] If `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` is renamed/shared, add/extend a drift guard so the delete/archive/unarchive/inspect `applies_to` stays pinned to the Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (same enforcement the clipboard trio has), so the TS set and Rust set cannot silently diverge.
-- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service` passes (run before/after: the new field-target assertions fail before the `applies_to` additions, pass after).
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the failing `list command` field-target assertions first, watch them fail (commands currently leak), then add the `applies_to` declarations to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -14,29 +14,25 @@

-## What
+## Resolution

-Give the four command registrations an `applies_to` capability set = the real cross-cutting entity types (task, tag, column, board, actor, project, attachment — the existing `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` / `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` set), which deliberately excludes `field` (and `view`/`perspective`, which have their own `perspective.*` commands). -- `builtin/plugins/entity-commands/index.ts` — add `applies_to: <real-entity-set>` to the `entity.delete` (~L24–43), `entity.archive` (~L50–69), and `entity.unarchive` (~L74–92) registrations. Keep the dispatch-time gating (the entity server's per-type `available()` impls) unchanged — this is purely the coarse list-level capability.
-- `builtin/plugins/app-shell-commands/commands/ui.ts` — add `applies_to: <same real-entity-set>` to the `app.inspect` registration (~L15–23). Leave `entity.inspect` (~L48–55) as is (already field-safe via server-side target resolution); add a one-line comment noting why it is intentionally ungated.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/src/caption.rs` — update the doc comment on `INSPECTABLE_ENTITY_PREFIXES` / `focused_entity_type` (≈L7–13) that currently states "a focused field … Fields remain inspectable via an explicit `target`", to reflect that field commands (inspect included) are now suppressed at the command surface.
-
-Scope note: this is the **command-surface (list-time)** suppression, matching how the clipboard leak was fixed. The direct double-click-an-`<Inspectable>`-field gesture is a separate webview dispatch path; if that should also stop inspecting fields, capture it as a follow-up task — it is out of scope here.
+- `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` renamed to `OPERABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` in `builtin/plugins/entity-commands/index.ts`, shared by the clipboard trio AND the CRUD trio (`entity.delete` / `entity.archive` / `entity.unarchive`), which all now declare `applies_to: OPERABLE_ENTITY_TYPES`.
+- `app.inspect` in `builtin/plugins/app-shell-commands/commands/ui.ts` declares the same set (local `OPERABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` const, mirrored + drift-guarded). `entity.inspect` left ungated with an explanatory comment.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/src/caption.rs` doc updated: field commands (inspect included) are now suppressed at the command surface.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] With a `field:` moniker as the focus (`ctx.target` = `field:task:01ABC.title`), `list command` does NOT return `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, or `app.inspect`.
-- [ ] With a real entity as the focus (`ctx.target` = `task:01ABC`), `list command` DOES return all four (no regression).
-- [ ] The clipboard trio behavior is unchanged; if `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` is renamed, the existing `assert_clipboard_applies_to` drift guard still passes.
-- [ ] No UI/React hardcoded `if (type === "field")` branch is introduced — gating stays declarative data on the registration, interpreted by `applies_to_focus`.
+- [x] With a `field:` moniker as the focus (`ctx.target` = `field:task:01ABC.title`), `list command` does NOT return `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, or `app.inspect`.
+- [x] With a real entity as the focus (`ctx.target` = `task:01ABC`), `list command` DOES return all four (no regression).
+- [x] The clipboard trio behavior is unchanged; `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` renamed to `OPERABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` and the existing `assert_clipboard_applies_to` drift guard still passes (delegates to the shared `assert_operable_applies_to`).
+- [x] No UI/React hardcoded `if (type === "field")` branch is introduced — gating stays declarative data on the registration, interpreted by `applies_to_focus`.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Extend the production-path e2e `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_entity_commands_e2e.rs` (it loads the REAL `entity-commands` + `app-shell-commands` bundles through a V8 isolate and reads surfaced `applies_to`): add a case driving `list command` with a `field:` target and asserting the four ids are absent, and with a `task:` target asserting they are present. Mirror the existing `assert_clipboard_applies_to` helper.
-- [ ] Add a focused unit case to `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/list_applies_to.rs` registering delete/archive/unarchive/inspect-shaped fixtures with the real-entity `applies_to` and asserting the field-target-suppressed / task-target-offered invariant through `applies_to_focus`.
-- [ ] If `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` is renamed/shared, add/extend a drift guard so the delete/archive/unarchive/inspect `applies_to` stays pinned to the Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (same enforcement the clipboard trio has), so the TS set and Rust set cannot silently diverge.
-- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service` passes (run before/after: the new field-target assertions fail before the `applies_to` additions, pass after).
+- [x] Extended the production-path e2e: `builtin_entity_commands_e2e::crud_commands_suppressed_on_a_field_offered_on_an_entity` (entity-commands bundle, CRUD trio) and `builtin_app_shell_commands_e2e::app_inspect_suppressed_on_a_field_offered_on_an_entity` (app-shell-commands bundle, app.inspect) — load the REAL bundles through the V8 isolate, drive `list command` with a `field:` target (four ids absent) and a `task:` target (present).
+- [x] Added focused unit cases to `tests/list_applies_to.rs`: `crud_inspect_commands_absent_when_a_field_is_the_context_menu_target` / `crud_inspect_commands_present_when_a_task_is_focused` (delete/archive/unarchive/inspect-shaped fixtures, field-suppressed / task-offered via `applies_to_focus`).
+- [x] Drift guard: `assert_operable_applies_to` pins each of delete/archive/unarchive `applies_to` (entity e2e) and `app.inspect` `applies_to` (app-shell e2e) to Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES`; `assert_clipboard_applies_to` retained (delegates) so the clipboard trio guard still passes; both anchor that `field`/`view`/`perspective` are excluded.
+- [x] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service` passes (field-target assertions FAILED before the `applies_to` additions, PASS after). The only remaining failure is the pre-existing, unrelated `meta_tree_id_param_is_required_where_expected` (confirmed failing without these changes).

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the failing `list command` field-target assertions first, watch them fail (commands currently leak), then add the `applies_to` declarations to make them pass. #field-moniker-fix #commands #entity-commands
\ No newline at end of file
+- Used `/tdd` — wrote the failing `list command` field-target assertions first, watched them fail (commands leaked), then added the `applies_to` declarations to make them pass. #field-moniker-fix #commands #entity-commands
\ No newline at end of file

Reuse the existing `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` constant, or rename it to a neutral name (e.g. `OPERABLE_ENTITY_TYPES`) shared by the clipboard trio and these three, since the set is identical. Keep the dispatch-time gating (the entity server's per-type `available()` impls) unchanged — this is purely the coarse list-level capability.
+- `builtin/plugins/app-shell-commands/commands/ui.ts` — add `applies_to: <same real-entity-set>` to the `app.inspect` registration (~L15–23). Leave `entity.inspect` (~L48–55) as is (already field-safe via server-side target resolution); add a one-line comment noting why it is intentionally ungated.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/src/caption.rs` — update the doc comment on `INSPECTABLE_ENTITY_PREFIXES` / `focused_entity_type` (≈L7–13) that currently states "a focused field … Fields remain inspectable via an explicit `target`", to reflect that field commands (inspect included) are now suppressed at the command surface.

-- `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` renamed to `OPERABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` in `builtin/plugins/entity-commands/index.ts`, shared by the clipboard trio AND the CRUD trio (`entity.delete` / `entity.archive` / `entity.unarchive`), which all now declare `applies_to: OPERABLE_ENTITY_TYPES`.
-- `app.inspect` in `builtin/plugins/app-shell-commands/commands/ui.ts` declares the same set (local `OPERABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` const, mirrored + drift-guarded). `entity.inspect` left ungated with an explanatory comment.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/src/caption.rs` doc updated: field commands (inspect included) are now suppressed at the command surface.
+Scope note: this is the **command-surface (list-time)** suppression, matching how the clipboard leak was fixed. The direct double-click-an-`<Inspectable>`-field gesture is a separate webview dispatch path; if that should also stop inspecting fields, capture it as a follow-up task — it is out of scope here.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [x] With a `field:` moniker as the focus (`ctx.target` = `field:task:01ABC.title`), `list command` does NOT return `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, or `app.inspect`.
-- [x] With a real entity as the focus (`ctx.target` = `task:01ABC`), `list command` DOES return all four (no regression).
-- [x] The clipboard trio behavior is unchanged; `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` renamed to `OPERABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` and the existing `assert_clipboard_applies_to` drift guard still passes (delegates to the shared `assert_operable_applies_to`).
-- [x] No UI/React hardcoded `if (type === "field")` branch is introduced — gating stays declarative data on the registration, interpreted by `applies_to_focus`.
+- [ ] With a `field:` moniker as the focus (`ctx.target` = `field:task:01ABC.title`), `list command` does NOT return `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, or `app.inspect`.
+- [ ] With a real entity as the focus (`ctx.target` = `task:01ABC`), `list command` DOES return all four (no regression).
+- [ ] The clipboard trio behavior is unchanged; if `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` is renamed, the existing `assert_clipboard_applies_to` drift guard still passes.
+- [ ] No UI/React hardcoded `if (type === "field")` branch is introduced — gating stays declarative data on the registration, interpreted by `applies_to_focus`.

 ## Tests
-- [x] Extended the production-path e2e: `builtin_entity_commands_e2e::crud_commands_suppressed_on_a_field_offered_on_an_entity` (entity-commands bundle, CRUD trio) and `builtin_app_shell_commands_e2e::app_inspect_suppressed_on_a_field_offered_on_an_entity` (app-shell-commands bundle, app.inspect) — load the REAL bundles through the V8 isolate, drive `list command` with a `field:` target (four ids absent) and a `task:` target (present).
-- [x] Added focused unit cases to `tests/list_applies_to.rs`: `crud_inspect_commands_absent_when_a_field_is_the_context_menu_target` / `crud_inspect_commands_present_when_a_task_is_focused` (delete/archive/unarchive/inspect-shaped fixtures, field-suppressed / task-offered via `applies_to_focus`).
-- [x] Drift guard: `assert_operable_applies_to` pins each of delete/archive/unarchive `applies_to` (entity e2e) and `app.inspect` `applies_to` (app-shell e2e) to Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES`; `assert_clipboard_applies_to` retained (delegates) so the clipboard trio guard still passes; both anchor that `field`/`view`/`perspective` are excluded.
-- [x] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service` passes (field-target assertions FAILED before the `applies_to` additions, PASS after). The only remaining failure is the pre-existing, unrelated `meta_tree_id_param_is_required_where_expected` (confirmed failing without these changes).
+- [ ] Extend the production-path e2e `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_entity_commands_e2e.rs` (it loads the REAL `entity-commands` + `app-shell-commands` bundles through a V8 isolate and reads surfaced `applies_to`): add a case driving `list command` with a `field:` target and asserting the four ids are absent, and with a `task:` target asserting they are present. Mirror the existing `assert_clipboard_applies_to` helper.
+- [ ] Add a focused unit case to `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/list_applies_to.rs` registering delete/archive/unarchive/inspect-shaped fixtures with the real-entity `applies_to` and asserting the field-target-suppressed / task-target-offered invariant through `applies_to_focus`.
+- [ ] If `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` is renamed/shared, add/extend a drift guard so the delete/archive/unarchive/inspect `applies_to` stays pinned to the Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (same enforcement the clipboard trio has), so the TS set and Rust set cannot silently diverge.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service` passes (run before/after: the new field-target assertions fail before the `applies_to` additions, pass after).

 ## Workflow
-- Used `/tdd` — wrote the failing `list command` field-target assertions first, watched them fail (commands leaked), then added the `applies_to` declarations to make them pass. #field-moniker-fix #commands #entity-commands
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing `list command` field-target assertions first, watch them fail (commands currently leak), then add the `applies_to` declarations to make them pass. #field-moniker-fix #commands #entity-commands
\ No newline at end of file

 - [x] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service` passes (field-target assertions FAILED before the `applies_to` additions, PASS after). The only remaining failure is the pre-existing, unrelated `meta_tree_id_param_is_required_where_expected` (confirmed failing without these changes).

 ## Workflow

\ No newline at end of file
+- Used `/tdd` — wrote the failing `list command` field-target assertions first, watched them fail (commands leaked), then added the `applies_to` declarations to make them pass. #field-moniker-fix #commands #entity-commands
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-14 13:20)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_app_shell_commands_e2e.rs:1501` and `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_entity_commands_e2e.rs:930` — Near-verbatim duplicated `assert_operable_applies_to` helper. Both functions have a byte-identical body (parse `applies_to` → sort → compare to sorted `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` → assert `view`/`perspective`/`field` absent); they differ ONLY in a leading comment and the `assert_eq!` message string. Why it matters: both modules are submodules of the SAME `integration` test binary and already share `tests/integration/support.rs`, where cross-bundle helpers conventionally live (`register_args`, `ids_of`, …). Two copies of the same drift-guard logic can silently drift (e.g. one updated to admit a new type, the other not) — exactly the divergence this guard exists to prevent. Suggestion: hoist one `pub(crate) fn assert_operable_applies_to(cmd, id)` into `support.rs` (it already has access to import `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` from `swissarmyhammer_kanban`), drop both local copies, and have `assert_clipboard_applies_to` delegate to it from there. Non-blocking: it is test scaffolding, the copies are currently identical, and both pin to the same Rust constant (which is itself the single source of truth), so production behavior is unaffected.
\ No newline at end of file
